schema.name: TZPID source truth generated from canonical equation master

identity.canonical_title: Short Worked Example (Symbolic Recipe You Can Use Right Away)

theory.canonical_statement: 1. Choose a carrier \(\omega_0\) and modulation frequency \(\omega_m\). If you want spatial nodal geometry with 6 spokes, choose an azimuthal index \(m=6\) (that maps to angular Bessel order \(J_6\)). 2. Choose a modulation index \(\kappa\) where \(J_6(\kappa)\) is large (sweep numerically). 3. Set creation kernel: \[ \lambda(x,t) = \lambda_0 \sum_n J_n(\kappa)^2 \exp\!\Big(-\frac{(\omega^{\rm beat}(x,t)-(\omega_0+n\omega_m))^2}{2\sigma^2}\Big)\big(1+\beta R(x,t)\big) \] 4. Evolve \(n(x,t)\) and \( \rho = m_0 n \); then compute potential via \(\nabla^2\Phi = 4\pi G_{\rm eff}\rho\) or use \(-\nabla n\) as short-range proxy for trap zones. 5. Observe whether the seed regions create large-scale aggregation and whether symmetry corresponds to the dominant n (e.g., n=6 → sixfold).
theory.technical_interpretation: Short Worked Example (Symbolic Recipe You Can Use Right Away) is an algebraic definition, written m=6. It is an algebraic definition expressing the quantity directly in terms of the variables on its right-hand side. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.

formation.note: Introduced in the derivation where if you want spatial nodal geometry with 6 spokes, choose an azimuthal index
